threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the Tube instead of just saying you're catching the Overground from the Dalston Junction.
Caught a peek at a guy in a floral shirt serenading a bus full of half-asleep commuters at 2 AM. Only in Peckham, right? Made my night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an old lady haggling over a watermelon on Sonnenallee, while a punk juggled in the background. Classic Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club event that starts at noon and still goes strong until Monday morning, with brunch specials served at 4 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they sidestep the BART delays—while newcomers look lost, the regulars know the shortcut to the bus.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 6 today, calmly reading Shakespeare. Just another Muni Tuesday, I guess.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals hang out at the big squares, but you'll find us tucked away in the little bars, especially on a Tuesday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still get off at Diagonal instead of taking the shortcut through the park to Gràcia. Classic rookie move.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can tell you're new if you still think the bus will actually arrive on time during a Tube strike. It's like waiting for a kettle to boil.
Last night on the 36, saw a guy arguing with a lamp post like it owed him money. The bus driver just shook his head and muttered 'Another night in Peckham.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a toddler in a tracksuit trying to negotiate with a stray cat over a half-eaten döner. Classic Neukölln life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a mix of Turkish döner shops and vegan cafes right across the street, both packed at 2 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they dodge the BART delays like it’s a game—everyone else just stands there confused, staring at the screens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog in a tiny blazer on the 14 this morning, looking more put-together than most of the humans. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has tapas, but some locals still prefer a simple bocadillo over all that fancy fare. We like our sandwiches straightforward.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their fascination with the 'vaca' on Carrer de Verdi—only locals know it’s gone and replaced with a cafe.
